# Rename a context through the CLI and assert per-context tags + alerts move
# with the rename rather than being stranded on the old name.

mkdir $WORK/kube
cp $WORK/prod.yaml $WORK/kube/prod.yaml

exec kcm tag palette add prod eu
exec kcm tag add prod prod eu --context prod-eu --dir $WORK/kube
exec kcm alert enable prod --context prod-eu --dir $WORK/kube

# Sanity: both present under the original name.
exec kcm tag list prod --context prod-eu --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'prod-eu'
stdout 'prod, eu'

exec kcm alert show prod --context prod-eu --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'Enabled:               true'

# Rename prod-eu → prod-europe (writes the kubeconfig file + moves state keys).
exec kcm context rename prod prod-eu prod-europe --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'renamed'

# The kubeconfig now references the new name.
exec kcm list --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'prod-europe'
! stdout 'prod-eu\b'

# Tags follow the rename.
exec kcm tag list prod --context prod-europe --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'prod-europe'
stdout 'prod, eu'

# Per-context alert survives the rename.
exec kcm alert show prod --context prod-europe --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'Enabled:               true'

# Old name no longer has state.
exec kcm tag list prod --context prod-eu --dir $WORK/kube
stdout 'no tags'

-- prod.yaml --
apiVersion: v1
kind: Config
current-context: prod-eu
clusters:
  - name: prod-eu-cluster
    cluster:
      server: https://prod-eu.example.test
contexts:
  - name: prod-eu
    context:
      cluster: prod-eu-cluster
      user: prod-admin
users:
  - name: prod-admin
    user:
      token: test
